name: Watercolor Studio
description: A quiet, beautiful place to paint with watercolors. Each placement is its own sheet of textured paper. Pigments are real watercolor pans — pick one, or add bits of several into the mixing well and they blend subtractively the way paint actually does (blue plus yellow makes green, complements make mud). A range of brushes (broad wash, round, flat, dry-brush, fine detail) lay translucent washes that pool, bleed, and darken at their edges; layered strokes glaze over one another. A clean-water Lift brush pulls pigment back out. Optional thin guide outlines (a pear, a teacup, a koi, a mountain lake…) sit faintly beneath the paint so new painters can trace as they learn. Editors in the space paint together in real time; anonymous viewers watch read-only. The whole painting is stored as deterministic vector strokes in a single JSON file per placement — no database, no network.
